How To Clean up Your Gmail

Learn how to efficiently manage your inbox by moving large volumes of messages at once. This guide will help you streamline your workflow and keep your email organized with ease.

1

Step 1:

Head to your Gmail inbox

2

Click the "select" button near the top left corner as shown

Alternatively you can search for specific emails you want to save. Find those emails (by sender, date, etc) and follow the same directions below starting on Step 4.

3

Click the "Select All Option"

4

You can choose to:

1. Mark them all as read (Red Circle)
2. Delete them all (Blue Circle)
3. Mark emails as red/important etc (Orange), see details below!
4. Add a label (Green Circle). Labeling emails as "Keep" will save these emails from being deleted automatically in the merge.

⚠️

Alert!

Gmail has slightly adjusted the menu bar for the options, they are still there but may be moved around slightly.

5

Assign all emails to a Label (similar to Outlook folders), select the label name or create a new folder.

💡

Tip!

If you already have emails organized in a folder, or want to save only a certain existing label/folder, the steps above can be done for just that group of emails by going into that folder/label and selecting them all.

⚠️
